name: Blake, Frank Orris Jr.
description: Frank is the original Economic shark, having gained most of his knowledge in the Rockies last summer, where he says he succeeded in locating several manganese mines. Hasn't any girl of his own, but causes Dosie much anxiety at times. Good all around athlete. Says he expects to make more money than any other man in the class, and we would not be surprised to see him do so.

name: Bodman, John Whittlesey
description: John is an earnest, conscientious student, but he squanders too much of his time in reading the Kansas City papers. Champion tennis player. Sings "Anna Held" songs with a graveyard face. Found time to contribute one ten-line article to the Rollamo. Slow moving, but gets there in the end, and we predict a rosy future for him.

name: Boland, Earl Frederick
description: For a while we were in doubt as to whether he would be with us on the all eventful day, but by a long hard pull and a determination not to be downed, he showed what was in him, and will make the finish. Always shows the proper school spirit. Rumored that he intends to go into the sign business. Is the prince of good fellows. Intends to return to New York after graduation, but we expect to see him succeed no matter where he goes.

name: Bowles, James Joseph
description: The "old reliable" football tackle. Specializes in Cyaniding. Is taking Petrography to help him on the farm, where he expects to go when he graduates. When he first came here used to do the society act, but has reformed of late. Is a sticker and the kind of a man you like to have with you in a tight place.

name: Branham, William Grover
description: Bill knows all about Joplin and is quite ready to impart his information. Came from Missouri University, and has made good. Bluffs out the Juniors in the Physics Lab. His attitude of finality ought to get him a job without any trouble, and what he has behind it ought to enable him to hold it without any trouble.

name: Bunten, James
description: Jimmy is the owner of a half interest in the American Eagle ranch, and his greatest ambition in life is to "rough up" Vogt. A genuinely whole-souled, good-hearted "old scout," from whom we will all regret parting. "Hello, thar," is his usual greeting. Don't worry about his future, he will take care of that, all right.

name: Burdick, Charles Adrian
description: Indefatigable snipe hunter. The cause of the "No Loafing" sign at the bakery. Undecided as to whether he will be an electrician or start a bakery of his own, but whatever he does he is bound to make good.

name: Caples, Russell Bigelow, Jr.
description: Cape has a fund of stories of "When I worked for the Government on the Tennessee," which are developed with mathematical accuracy, no detail being omitted. Troubled with bashfulness, but it is hoped that his recently awakened interest in one of the fair sex will cure him. Has the faculty of doing lots of work in a short space of time. Is one of the "guns" of the class and always "Johnny-on-the-spot" when it comes to a show-down.

name: Clark, John Charles
clubs: Formerly vice president of the Y. M. C. A.
description: Chief occupation is competing with "The Midshipman." When not fussing can be found at the cigar store discussing learnedly on trap shooting, good tobacco, and the merits of Rolla as a summer resort. His ability is unquestioned, and should win him a high place in the world.

name: Connelly, Harry Wade
description: That his answers in class usually commence with "why, why-a," and that quite frequently that is as far as he gets, is no sign that Wade doesn't know. He is only trying to fool the Profs. Comes from Kansas, and is the happiest man in school, presumably because he got away from there. Wears a khaki coat, on which he has rented advertising space to Caples, Boland and Bowles. Is everybody's friend and ought to make good.

name: Detweiler, Alfred Nicks
description: Ran a country store before he came to school and must have lost some of his voice then, for you wonder how so big a man can have so small a voice. Good football player, good student and good all around man. If he works as hard after he gets out of school as he has in, he sure will make a success.

name: Diaz, Emilio
description: Is a wide awake Chilean who "savvied" American ways readily, and has made good with a vengeance. Is fond of hearing and telling a good joke, and can laugh louder than any man in the class. He hasn't missed an exempt list since he can remember. Will return to Chile when he graduates, and will be a credit to the school.

name: Dosenbach, Benjamin Harrison
description: Has had trouble with the girl question and with his studies, but is now doing well with both. If he can slide into a good job as easily as he can into second base he has a successful future before him. Will be remembered by all for his good nature and generosity.

name: Farrar, Monroe
description: Farrar started out as a farmer and then decided to try mining instead, but we expect to see him go back to the farm in the end. Has always taken an interest in athletics, and has done good work in the distances. Is very quiet and subdued, but when occasion demands it will come out of his trance long enough to say what he thinks. He chaperones Traughber and McNutt, and makes them lead the simple life.

name: Forman, John Kavanaugh
description: So conscientious and attentive in class that he is a ready reference in looking up lesson assignments. At the stock room always asks for "Judigers." Is reported to have a girl out Salem road. Recommends the use of forced draft for blast furnaces. Whatever he does he does well and this quality will bring him success.

name: Fraser, Keith Colt
description: He is so quiet and unobtrusive that his presence was not even suspected until he had been here over a year. Fusses intermittently. Proved to be the long-distance walker of the Colorado trip. Is a good, steady, hard-working fellow, and has made many friends. Will be remembered as a man who kept out of the limelight from preference and not from lack of ability.

name: Flynt, Frank Leroy
description: He is a remnant of the '08 Class, and so we don't know much about him, but since he has been with us he has demonstrated that he is one of the best men they have left behind. Spends most of his time at home with his wife and children.

name: Gregory, Clay, Jr.
description: Probably the best all-around athlete in school. His marked ability to do a term's work in the last week has saved him many times from the "lucrative position" route. Originated the "cuckoo" yell, and taught Charlie how to say "Um-ma-lay-a-toe." Has remarkable ability along all lines, and if he doesn't make good we don't know who will.

name: Harlan, John Dee
description: Jack has a girl in every town in which he has ever been, although that doesn't seem to keep him from getting out his work on time. Has the ability of getting good grades without doing much studying. Worked at the Liberty Bell Mine, and is always ready to tell all about it. Is a hustler, and as a promoter, well he could make a go out of a placer mine in the middle of the Sahara Desert. Thoroughness and stick-to-it-iveness are his characteristics, and will bring him success.

name: Holmes, Oliver Wendell
description: Wanted over a year so he could graduate with the Class of 1910. Helps take care of the Count when he meets with disappointments in his love affair. Quiet and unobtrusive, but is always there with the goods.

name: Jones, William Hamilton
description: Believes that if four years of college are good, five years are better, and it is rumored that he is beginning to believe that six years are best. Has carried a diamond ring around for four years, but seems unable to unload it. We wish him better luck in the future. Once made an E in assaying. Bill doesn't believe in letting his school work interfere with his course in "Girl-ology," but, taken all in all, is a mighty good fellow, even though he is in love.

name: Karte, Anton Frederick
description: Dutch got a late start and so won't be able to stand up with us when we receive our sheepskins in June. However, he will come in strong at the finish next year. Is always good-natured and has many friends.

name: Kenney, John Richardson
description: His main grievance being that the Frisco won't stop their fast trains there. Would have made good on the football team if he had only developed speed, learned to tackle properly, and learned to catch and carry the ball. Was a star on the Y. M. C. A. team. Jack is a born electrician, and will make a brilliant success at his chosen profession, and this, combined with his mining experience, should make him a valuable man in the field.

name: Killian, Ralph Daniel
description: A quiet, progressive, square-dealing fellow, the kind you would trust to take care of your money, if you were fortunate to possess some of the long green. Spends most of his spare time taking care of Pete. Always has a rather serious expression, which he uses to good effect in fooling the Profs. The sort of a fellow that you would like to have for a next door neighbor.

name: McNutt, Vachel Harry
description: McNutt, McNutt, shades of Scotland, what a name for a "show-me" Missourian. You listen for the whistle and drone of bag-pipes to say that the "Campbells are coming," but all you hear is the rustle and sigh of the wind through his whiskers to announce the arrival of McNutt. Mac is assistant to Cox, and can grow more hair on his chin than Cox can on his head. Is a born Mineralogist, and our only hope for some day claiming the U. S. G. S. Chief.

name: Mackey, Robert William
description: Is one of the promoters of the "Rolla Taxicab Co.," but spends most of his spare time in "close harmony" for the benefit of the fair sex, and in teaching Chili how to swear in English. Has had numerous love affairs, but so far none have proved fatal. Is an enthusiastic fisherman and an authority on all matters pertaining to that sport. Is the Editor-in-Chief of this book, and so modest that he won't let any mention be made of his good qualities.

name: Minor, Harmon Edward
description: Comes from under the shadow of the prison walls, and has turned out well considering his environments. We do not know whether he was ever an inmate or not, but as he has never done much but work and mind his own business we will give him the benefit of the doubt and will say no more about it. Falls in love for a few weeks the beginning of the third term each year. Will probably be a civil engineer when he gets out, and we see no reason why he should not reach a high mark with little difficulty.

name: Morgan, Allen Ray Dearborn
description: Now "Mr. Morgan of Rolla, Mo." Although Rube is not finishing up with us we know that it is not due to lack of effort on his part. Is a natural leader and organizer, and can make a good speech at any gathering from Epworth League to a Strawberry Social. Is a bright fellow and a good mixer, two qualities that will assure him a hearty reception wherever he goes.

name: Murphy, Benton Franklin
description: "Pat" is the old reliable of the Senior class. Always the one best bet and the man who knows when all other sources fail. Helped do the tramming for the Miners' Minstrel and as a reward was allowed to act as scene shifter for the Girls' Minstrel. He is a good fellow anywhere and a high card with the ladies. Is one of the guns of the class and if any man in the class is to make a big success, he is more than likely to be that man.

name: Owen, Harvey Skidmore
motto: "I never let my school work interfere with my college education."
description: Will not graduate this year, but requested that his picture be put in so he could show it to the folks. Nobody doubts his ability and he probably would have gotten through had it not been for his numerous love affairs.

name: Park, Albert
description: A man of many and varied interests. Father of the Y. M. C. A. A regular "Dudley second" when it comes to asking fool questions in the class room. His many friends will remember him as one of the hardest working and most conscientious members of the class.

name: Peterson, Howard Kelsey
description: Says he came to Rolla because his mother was afraid for him to take the train each day to go into New York City to school. We understand that he has a school teacher picked out down in the swamps of southeast Missouri, and that soon after Commencement we may expect the invites. Toward the end of the term displays a great interest in the exempt lists and manages to make some of them, too. Thinks that half an hour is ample time to solve any problem.

name: Pickering, John Lyle Jr.
description: Came from the University of Illinois two years ago, and his presence has been a treat to the girls. How they will miss him when he is gone. Advocated the use of tube-mills as coarse crushers. His steadiness and ability assure him a place in the engineering world.

name: Porri, Louis Joseph
description: His main object in the course seems to be to keep on the good side of the profs. and of Clay Gregory, and at this much, at least, he is an adept. Is one of the best students in his class, but on trips can ask more fool questions in five minutes than can all the rest of the class in a whole day. Recommends that air-compressors be used to run generators. Spends a great portion of his time chaperoning his small brother. His brilliant intellect will bring him much credit, if he applies it rightly.

name: Reide, Fred Edward
description: Noted as a non-fusser, as during his four years has never been to see a girl. Dutch has always been a hard, consistent worker and an interested participant in all student and athletic affairs. He has always given the best that is in him and has, by his efforts and labors, assured the success of many student movements.

name: Schmidt, Sidney Randolph
description: Sid comes from Chicago and does his best to uphold the reputation of the Windy City. However, his talk is quite worth while, as he generally knows pretty well what he is talking about. Has recently reformed, there being the usual reason. Sid is a hard worker when he gets started and has done more work for this book than have five of the members of the Board. Once made an E and three F's on the same geology paper. Is a good all around man, the possessor of a good brain and great originality and we expect that he will climb the ladder to success in short order.

name: Schultz, John Elmer
description: Celebrated his initiation into Tau Beta Pi by "conning" Dynamo. Was a newspaper reporter before he came here to school, and takes his notes in shorthand. But no matter whether he is an engineer or goes back to the newspaper, we are confident he will meet with success.

name: Stewart, John Sloan Jr.
description: The life of the Colorado trip. Always on the lookout for dances. Is a general favorite with the ladies and a society dog of the first water. When he first came here he had a girl at Mansfield, but now— His originality has never been questioned and in all we predict a brilliant future. Always wears a smile upon his face and will never be without friends.

name: Smith, Harvey Edson
description: Used to be quite a fusser, but this year has joined the "stay-at-home club." A prime favorite of Prof. Copeland's when it comes to oral quizzes. Has wonderful ability to make the profs believe that although present in body he is absent in spirit and then wilfully hurt the profs' feelings by answering correctly the questions asked him. Has made several trips to Columbia this year for some unexplained reason. He is a hustler and one of the guns of the class. Has unquestioned ability and will make good.

name: Smith, Van Hoose
description: Native of Arkansas and by the grace of God and much good luck will be with us next June. Helped Harvey water the horse his first year, but since then has been in social retirement. However, it is understood that he has a "queen" down in Arkansas. Is one of the most popular men in the class and a friend of everyone.

name: Thompson, Reuben Conrad
description: An all around athlete, student and good fellow. Trammed for the Miners' Minstrel. Fusses very intermittently, but we understand that one of the "younger set" is "simply crazy" about him. The kind of a fellow that is bound to succeed in whatever he undertakes. Our only regret is that Tommy was not with us for the full four years.

name: Traughber, Charles Weaver
description: Our almost human grasshopper or better, our human almost grasshopper, on account of his ability in negotiating the broad jump. Is sometimes mistaken for a Frenchman, but he isn't. He is from Missouri. He has no girl in Rolla, but he has a girl in pretty nearly every other town in which he has ever been. Always good natured and has a good word for everyone. He has plenty of ability and good common sense, as well, and will succeed wherever he may be placed.

name: Vogt, George C.
description: During his senior year he became a member of the St. James Fussing Association, which kept him up oftentimes until 4 a. m. When he wasn't at St. James he found plenty of time to sleep, some to study and always had enough time left to be sociable and accommodating. Is a chronic roughhouser. Upon graduation will return to dear old Iowa. George has made many friends at school and we shall hate to part from him in June, but whatever he does or wherever he goes we know we shall hear from him, as he is bound to make good.

name: Wander, Ernest
description: Addicted to long walks out Salem road. Believes that a course in Girlology is an essential part of a mining engineer's education. Always willing to help with what he knows and knowing it, too, has made him a general favorite with his classmates.
